name: Key Symbols Guide
summary: The sacred scriptures and writings that form the foundation of Christian faith and theology.
structure: Interpretive Framework - Key symbols and their meanings in Revelation
influence: Central to understanding apocalyptic literature and prophetic interpretation
icon:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 64 64">
  <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="28.8" fill="#9370DB" opacity="0.2"/>
  <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="19.2" fill="none" stroke="#9370DB" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,5"/>
  <text x="32" y="44.8" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22.4" fill="#9370DB">✨</text>
</svg>
description: Key Symbols Guide is an important sacred text in christian tradition.
author: John (Apostle)

keyMyths.title: The Crucifixion of Jesus
keyMyths.description: Jesus's crucifixion on the cross is the central event in Christian theology, representing his sacrifice for the sins of humanity and paving the way for salvation. It signifies redemption and the promise of eternal life for believers.
keyMyths.source: Gospels of Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John

keyMyths.title: The Resurrection of Jesus
keyMyths.description: Three days after his crucifixion, Jesus rose from the dead, demonstrating his victory over death and sin. This event is foundational to Christian faith, affirming the possibility of resurrection and eternal life for believers.
keyMyths.source: Gospels of Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John; 1 Corinthians 15

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Adam and Eve
keyMyths.description: God created Adam and Eve, the first humans, placing them in the Garden of Eden. Their disobedience by eating the forbidden fruit led to the Fall of Man, introducing sin and suffering into the world.

keyMyths.title: The Great Flood (Noah's Ark)
keyMyths.description: God, grieved by humanity's wickedness, sent a great flood to cleanse the Earth, saving only Noah, his family, and pairs of animals aboard the Ark. The story symbolizes God's judgment and the promise of a new beginning.

keyMyths.title: The Parable of the Prodigal Son
keyMyths.description: A son squanders his inheritance but returns home repentant, where his father welcomes him with open arms. This parable illustrates God's unconditional love, forgiveness, and the joy of repentance.

sources.name: The Holy Bible
sources.description: The foundational text of Christianity, comprising the Old and New Testaments.
sources.title: The Holy Bible

sources.name: The Catechism of the Catholic Church
sources.description: A comprehensive summary of Catholic doctrine and teachings.
sources.title: The Catechism of the Catholic Church

sources.name: Augustine of Hippo, *Confessions*
sources.description: An autobiographical work that explores themes of sin, grace, and conversion, influential in Christian theology.
sources.title: Augustine of Hippo, *Confessions*

sources.name: Thomas Aquinas, *Summa Theologica*
sources.description: A systematic theological treatise that synthesizes Aristotelian philosophy with Christian doctrine.
sources.title: Thomas Aquinas, *Summa Theologica*

sources.name: Elaine Pagels, *The Gnostic Gospels*
sources.description: A scholarly work exploring the diversity of early Christian beliefs through the lens of Gnostic texts.
sources.title: Elaine Pagels, *The Gnostic Gospels*
